title: Fake Depth Signature Context
focus_questions: Did the displayed depth survive near touch, or disappear when it mattered?
Was there real same-price defense, or only a visual depth signature?

summary.thesis: Displayed depth becomes unreliable when it repeatedly cancels near touch, fails to defend, and coexists with opposite-side real fills.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trust a bright wall or bid until it survives near touch and produces real outcome.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when live depth looks important but the behavior of that depth is more deceptive than supportive.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: visible depth near a decision zone. Required evidence: repeated near-touch cancellation, elevated pull rate, or opposite-side fills while the displayed side disappears. Do not trade: from screen brightness alone or by mislabeling cosmetic depth as replenishment. Real trigger: repeated depth failure to survive or defend. Failure point: the depth starts surviving near touch, reloading, and producing real defense.

when_relevant: Large displayed size repeatedly appears near an active level.
The operator is tempted to infer support or resistance from visible depth alone.
Lifecycle evidence still needs to decide whether the depth is reliable.
required_evidence: significant displayed size is visible near price
there are enough observations to judge survival near touch
price outcome after the displayed depth appears can be reviewed
confirmation_signals: large displayed size repeatedly disappears near touch
opposite-side fills occur while the displayed side fails to stand in
visible size does not create the reaction that a defended level should create
invalidation_signals: the displayed depth survives near touch and repeatedly produces defense
same-price replenishment begins proving true control instead of cosmetic depth
price outcome starts matching a real defended level
no_trade_conditions: Do not lean on the first few price levels from brightness alone.
Do not call same-price replenishment if the size mostly cancels before pressure arrives.
Do not fade price only because a large wall appears if that wall has not produced outcome.

entry_archetypes.name: post-cancel continuation
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after the supposed wall or bid repeatedly disappears and price continues through the area with no real defense.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the level still has unresolved counterflow and no clean outcome yet.

entry_archetypes.name: repeated failed visual defense
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after another attempt by visible depth fails again to produce the expected defense.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the latest test actually begins to hold.

management_notes: Repeated cancellation behavior is more valuable than one isolated flash.
This card should hand off into continuation or failure cards once real outcome appears.
Visible size without survival near touch should be downgraded fast.
review_questions: Did the displayed depth survive near touch?
Was the level real structure or only visual bait?
Did the operator trade the screen image instead of the actual outcome?
